heading: Presumption of contract for disposition of personalty
label: 8521 Presumption of contract for disposition of personalty
text: The fact of death of a veteran (admitted as such), or a dependent or survivor of a veteran receiving care under the penultimate sentence of , in a facility or hospital, while being furnished care or treatment therein by the Department, leaving no spouse, next of kin, or heirs, shall give rise to a conclusive presumption of a valid contract for the disposition in accordance with this subchapter, but subject to its conditions, of all property described in owned by said decedent at death and as to which such person dies intestate.section 1781(b) of this titlesection 8520 of this title
notes: Amendments2002— substituted “the penultimate sentence of section 1781(b)” for “the last sentence of section 1713(b)”.Pub. L. 107–1351991— renumbered as this section and substituted “8520” for “5220”.Pub. L. 102–40section 5221 of this titlesubstituted “1713(b)” for “613(b)” and “Department” for “Veterans’ Administration”.Pub. L. 102–831986— substituted “such person” for “he”.Pub. L. 99–5761976— inserted “, or a dependent or survivor of a veteran receiving care under the last sentence of ,” after “(admitted as such)”.Pub. L. 94–581section 613(b) of this title
Effective Date of 1976 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 94–581section 211 of Pub. L. 94–581section 111 of this titleOct. 21, 1976
